name: manim-gl
description: Creates an animated explainer video with ManimGL, the OpenGL-based 3Blue1Brown engine (manimlib import, manimgl CLI). Plans a storyboard from a described concept, writes a ManimGL scene, and renders it headless to MP4. Use this skill ONLY when the user explicitly asks for ManimGL, manimgl, manimlib, the 3Blue1Brown engine, or 3b1b — for any other animation request the manim-ce skill is the default. Covers math derivations, algorithm walkthroughs, concept explainers, function and data plots, 3D camera scenes, and title cards rendered through the ManimGL pipeline.

inputs.name: concept
inputs.description: Plain-language description of what to animate or explain

inputs.name: duration
inputs.description: Target video length in seconds; bot picks ~20-40s from complexity if absent

inputs.name: quality
inputs.description: Render quality low|medium|high; defaults to medium (720p30)

inputs.name: aspect
inputs.description: Frame aspect landscape|portrait|square; defaults to landscape (16:9)

inputs.name: style-notes
inputs.description: Optional look-and-feel notes (background color, accent color, mood)

outputs.name: video
outputs.description: The rendered animation as an H.264 MP4

outputs.name: scene-source
outputs.description: The ManimGL scene that produced the video

outputs.name: summary
outputs.description: Storyboard beats, engine and settings used, and suggested next edits
